party_a.current_emotional_state: composed_and_strategically_positioned — maintaining procedural initiative while appearing cooperative

emotional_arc.assessment: composed but vigilant — asserting a procedural boundary while managing relational tone
composed_and_strategically_measured — maintaining diplomatic tone while holding a firm procedural position
composed_and_strategically_positioned — maintaining procedural initiative while appearing cooperative

accumulated_interests.interest: accuracy and verifiability in determining what debts exist and their amounts
protection against agreements based on incomplete or inaccurate information
maintaining a working relationship with Party B (evidenced by diplomatic framing)
factual accuracy and documentary verification before committing to any agreement
protection against being bound by incomplete or disputed recollections
maintaining appearance of reasonableness and good faith (may be genuine or strategic)
establishing a procedural framework that favors their position (likely has stronger documentation)
avoiding being bound by disputed or incomplete recollections
establishing factual baseline before negotiating split
maintaining appearance of reasonableness while controlling process frame

risk_signal_history.signal: mild — possible concern about being rushed into agreement without adequate information
mild — reference to 'different recollections' suggests awareness of potential factual disputes
mild: strategic framing that positions their request as reasonable while potentially foreclosing Party B's alternative narrative
mild: information advantage — likely knows what documents will show
mild: has successfully positioned their frame as the only frame without Party B contesting it
mild: may have information advantage regarding what documentation will show

relational_posture_progression.posture: assertive but conciliatory — setting a boundary while explicitly preserving Party B's good faith
assertive_but_diplomatic — has successfully set the procedural frame while preserving surface cooperation
assertive_but_diplomatic — has set agenda across six turns while preserving surface cooperation

scaffold_divergence_log.model_value: unknown — no interaction has occurred between parties yet

Party A has made an opening move that is both substantive (procedural requirement) and relational (charitable attribution). Party B's response will reveal whether this framing is accepted, contested, or generative. Currently one-sided communication with no reaction data.

asymmetric_with_Party_A_controlling_frame — Party A has established both the substantive issue (disputed recollection requiring documentation) and the procedural remedy (review before agreement). Party B has accepted this frame while expressing self-doubt. The 'understanding' Party B mentioned is an unexplored thread that may contain their only independent interest articulation. Risk: process becomes validation of whoever has better records rather than genuine bilateral interest exploration.

asymmetric_with_unchallenged_frame — Party A has comprehensively defined both the problem (disputed recollections) and the solution (document review first) while Party B has only provided logistics. The 'understanding' Party B mentioned at T4 was the only potential independent thread and was immediately retracted with self-doubt rather than explained. This is not normal bilateral mediation — one party is driving and the other is following without ever stating a destination.

notes: Turn 1 represents the pre-mediation state — no party input exists to assess
Scaffold perception context reflects system defaults rather than observed signals
Cannot meaningfully assess emotional state, interests, or relational posture without party speech
First substantive perception pass will occur after at least one party speaks
Party A's 'good faith but inaccurate' formulation is doing double work — asserting factual disagreement while protecting the relational field
The request to 'look at statements' before agreeing is a process anchor, not yet a substantive position on what the debts are or how to split them
Party B's silence is purely structural (they haven't been invited yet) — no inference about their state is warranted
The perception agent note about epistemological authority is critical — this is about whose version of facts will ground the negotiation

Party B's statement 'we already talked about this and had an understanding' is substantively important but was immediately abandoned — this may be the only window into Party B's independent view of what was agreed

Party B's acceptance of documentation review came with self-doubt ('genuinely uncertain whether their recollection is fully accurate') — this could be genuine or could reflect pressure to appear reasonable by accepting Party A's frame

The current trajectory — moving to document review without first hearing Party B's substantive position — would be procedurally premature and risks validating information advantage rather than exploring interests
Party A's diplomatic tone is effective strategic framing but should not obscure that only one party has articulated substantive interests
The domain reasoner's CATEGORY 1 safety veto is correct — Party B's pattern is qualitatively different from normal responsive participation
Party B has had multiple explicit invitations to speak (T3 invitation to respond, T5 direct prompt to explain the 'understanding') and has not used any of them to claim independent ground
Moving to document review logistics now would effectively end interest exploration with only one party's interests on record
The mediator must interrupt the procedural momentum Party A has established and create unmissable space for Party B to articulate substantive position

party_b.current_emotional_state: uncertain_and_substantively_absent — has participated logistically but not claimed any independent ground on what they actually want

emotional_arc.assessment: uncertain_and_possibly_deferential — expressing self-doubt about own recollection, has not articulated independent position
uncertain_and_substantively_absent — has participated logistically but not claimed any independent ground on what they actually want

possible: preserving the 'understanding' they referenced but did not elaborate
possible: avoiding conflict or appearing unreasonable by contesting Party A's frame
CRITICAL GAP: no substantive interests articulated across six turns
possible: avoiding conflict by accepting Party A's frame
possible: the unexplored 'understanding' from T4 may contain their only independent position
unknown: what they believe the debts actually are or what split they think is fair

risk_signal_history.signal: moderate: has not articulated any independent position across four turns — only reactive statements
moderate: self-doubt about own recollection may reflect genuine uncertainty OR relational deference
moderate: accepting Party A's procedural frame without stating own needs or concerns
moderate: potential information asymmetry — may have weaker documentation or less clear recollection
moderate-to-high: compliance-only pattern — has not stated what they want from this resolution
moderate: self-doubt expression may reflect genuine uncertainty OR relational deference pattern
moderate: six turns of reactive participation without initiating any substantive content
moderate: accepting procedural frame that may not serve their interests

compliant_and_reactive — logistically cooperative but substantively silent
